metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1925
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Authors investigate the role of Chmp2b by using a control or Chmp2b-targeting shRNA. Hippocampal 16DIV cultures were transfected with these constructs and neurons were analyzed 2 days later. Depletion of Chmp2b induced a reduction of the density of postsynaptic spines, an effect that was mimicked by a Chmp2b construct unable to bind endogenous Chmp4b, which suggests the involvement of both proteins in the process. These data was supported by a reduction in the frequency of mEPSCs in neurons transfected with the Chmp2b-shRNA.
Moreover, Golgi impregnations of CA1 neurons from WT and KD mice, showed that the total density of spines was significatively reduced in KD animals, which was further confirmed by counting asymmetric synapses in electro micrographs of the hippocampus striatum radiatum.

18/11/2017 Pim
- Fig.8 shows the involvement of Chmp2b in the stimulus dependent (cLTP) change in spine morphology.. Experimental description: M&M:
-Pregnant Oncins France souche A (OFA; a substrain of Sprague Dawley) rats (Charles River) were used for primary neuron cultures, and 2-month-old OFA rats of both sexes were used for biochemical analysis of brain tissue.
-The pSuper-mCherry vector was described by Belly et al. (2010) and expresses the inserted shRNA together with mCherry. The Chmp2b-specific shRNA (Lee et al., 2007; Belly et al., 2010) and the GFP-specific shRNA [previously shown by Alvarez et al. (2006).
-Three-month-old male C57BL/6 mice and 1.3-month-old female Chmp2b KO mice (here called KD mice since knock-out is incomplete; Ghazi-Noori et al., 2012) or female WT littermates (on C57BL/6 background) were used for immunohistochemistry and electron microscopy (EM) studies.

From Berry et al., 2010:
The shRNA targeted the rat CHMP2B sequence at positions 578-596 (5'-GACACTACAAACAATGCAG-3') according to Lee et al. (Lee et al., 2007) and the encoding oligonucleotide was cloned between the HindIII and BglII sites of pSuper-mCherry.

18/11/2017
- Overexpressed Chmp2b protien is of human species. Traced back to PMID:20699355: " The wild-type human CHMP2B cDNA was cloned from HeLa cells by RT-PCR..."
